title: LLM prompt-rewrite and agent-suggestion are dashboard-only, advisory, and deferred
context: An internal-LLM capability was proposed: rewriting prompts and suggesting which LLM/agent should take each task. This runs into the spec's Non-Goals (§3): Waystation is 'not a general LLM prompting framework' and 'not an autonomous agent runner'. The concern is keeping the core ledger local-first and fully functional with no API key configured.
decision: LLM features live in the dashboard only, as explicit human-triggered, advisory actions ('rewrite this prompt', 'suggest an agent for this task') that stream results. They are not baked into core brief/prompt-render logic, never run automatically, and never dispatch work. The dashboard performs them via the same core write path (spec §12), writing results back as normal records/edits. LLM API configuration lives in a dashboard-specific config block, so `waystation init` and CLI/MCP usage never require an API key. This is deferred past V1 (future integration, spec §22).
consequences: Core CLI/MCP stays local-first with zero LLM dependency.
Feature is opt-in and isolated to the dashboard; no Non-Goals violation.
Streaming responses favor the JS dashboard stack (Hono SSE + React), consistent with the chosen Bun stack.
Deferred: not part of the V1 milestone scope.
